highlight.title: Next.js 16.2: Turbopack Stable with 400% Faster Dev Server
highlight.summary: <cite index="10-2,10-15,7-15,7-16">Next.js 16.2 introduces stable Turbopack builds with Server Fast Refresh, 400% faster dev server performance, and comprehensive AI developer tools including experimental Agent DevTools for terminal access to React DevTools.</cite>

resources.title: React Compiler v1.0: Automatic Memoization Goes Stable
resources.why_now: <cite index="27-6,27-7,27-8">The React Compiler's first stable release eliminates manual useMemo and useCallback optimization, automatically optimizing components and hooks through build-time memoization, now battle-tested on major Meta apps.</cite>

resources.title: Full-Stack TypeScript Pattern Evolution: From T3 Stack to Zero-Codegen
resources.why_now: <cite index="35-7,35-8,35-9">The T3 stack (Next.js + TypeScript + tRPC + Prisma/Drizzle + Tailwind) became the dominant pattern for full-stack TypeScript, providing end-to-end type safety from database schema to UI components without manual annotations.</cite>

resources.title: tRPC v12: Total Type Safety Without REST Boundaries
resources.why_now: <cite index="34-11,34-12,34-25">tRPC v12 eliminates the frontend-backend boundary with compile-time type inference, zero runtime cost validation, and native React Server Components support, making the "String-Based API" approach obsolete in 2026.</cite>

resources.title: Next.js 15 vs 16: Breaking Changes and Migration Strategy
resources.why_now: <cite index="8-2,8-3,8-6">Next.js versions 15 and 16 represent the most significant updates since App Router introduction, with Turbopack delivering 10x faster builds and Partial Prerendering enabling hybrid static-dynamic rendering patterns.</cite>

claude_take: <cite index="17-22,17-23,17-24">2026 marks TypeScript as the baseline for professional React development, with full-stack type safety becoming non-negotiable through tools like tRPC and server functions that blur client-server boundaries.</cite> <cite index="27-19,4-1,4-2">The React Compiler's stable release and Next.js's performance leap signal a maturation phase where manual optimization becomes automated, allowing developers to focus on features rather than performance tuning.</cite>
